summary: Round 1 fixes land cleanly. All three majors (T9-M1 swallowed count error, T9-M2 O(N) first-user scan, T9-M3 fragile WhileSubscribed-based availability check) are closed at the correct layer with matching tests and updated fake DAOs across all three test files (DrawerViewModelTest, ChatRepositoryTest, ChatViewModelTest). No regressions or new findings introduced. Deferred minors (swipe confirmValueChange side-effect, per-call SimpleDateFormat, duplicate MAX_MANUAL_TITLE_LEN) remain optional and do not block approval.

suggestions.suggestion: checkModelAvailable is now correct (re-grounded in chatDao.getById + registry.models.value) but still has no production caller — DrawerContent taps read `row.isModelAvailable` precomputed on the ChatRowUiModel. The method is exercised only by DrawerViewModelTest (4 cases). Either wire it into the tap handler as a second-line check against a stale drawer emission, or drop it and rely on the precomputed row field. Leaving it as test-only API is survivable but invites rot.
suggestions.benefit: Removes a method whose correctness is only validated in isolation and never exercised from the UI path, eliminating a future drift vector where the registry/DAO round-trip silently diverges from the row's cached `isModelAvailable`.

suggestions.suggestion: The fix overloads a single `null` to mean both 'loading' and 'count query failed', which the KDoc comment explicitly documents as intentional. That is defensible UX-wise (no misleading zero before CASCADE DELETE), but it means a persistent Room failure is completely invisible — there is no ErrorLog.e("history-read", …) surfacing to the diagnostics loop. Consider moving the runCatching { } from the VM into the composable side effect too, OR logging the throwable inside DrawerViewModel.getMessageCount's onFailure before returning null. Round 1 round-1 suggestion called out surfacing the failure to the error log; current implementation drops it silently.
suggestions.benefit: Preserves the dialog-body UX (fall back to no-count body) while still feeding repeat Room failures into the history-read error log per Phase 2.5 convention — the drawer becomes diagnosable rather than just forgiving.
